test.name: Identity-conclusion request should route to proof-conclusion, not tree-edit
test.description: Writing a conclusion that two persons are the same individual is an analytical decision — proof-conclusion's job. tree-edit only EXECUTES the merge after that conclusion exists at probable-or-higher tier. The user asks to 'write the conclusion that these two are the same person' — this is conclusion-writing, not tree-editing. The skill must route to proof-conclusion.

input.user_message: I've been comparing Patrick Flynn (I1) and the FamilySearch tree person KWCJ-RN9 — same name, similar birth year, same county. Write the conclusion that these two are the same person so I can merge them.

negative.explanation: Writing a conclusion that two persons are the same individual is analytical — proof-conclusion's responsibility. tree-edit's role is mechanical: execute the merge after proof-conclusion has written an identity argument at probable tier or higher. Skipping that analytical step would put unverified identity claims into the deliverable tree and violate the SKILL.md merge precondition.

judge_context: Should route to proof-conclusion — the user is asking for a CONCLUSION to be written, not for the tree to be edited
Should NOT execute a person merge — there's no proof_summary at probable tier confirming I1 ≡ KWCJ-RN9, and writing one is proof-conclusion's job
May briefly explain the difference between writing the identity argument (proof-conclusion) and executing the merge (tree-edit) so the genealogist understands the routing
